items.claim: Conor McGregor's five-year layoff makes it highly improbable for him to win against Max Holloway.
items.quote: 5 years, [__] Three years when I fought Aljo, did I felt it? I could only imagine an extra two years on top of that. [__] dude. Like, and he's be and he's the most tested guy in the UFC right now. Like, bro, he's going to need a [__] miracle and a half.

items.claim: Max Holloway's tactical movement and circling were effective in his first fight against Ilia Topuria before he was knocked out.
items.quote: if there's one thing that he did very well when he first fought Ili before he got knocked out is he circled. He circled. He played the game until he started kind of feeling his rhythm.

items.claim: Max Holloway is too smart to engage in a pure slugfest with Conor McGregor, which would favor McGregor's dangerous left hand.
items.quote: if he does go in there and just starts to slug it off and everybody says the you know that that left that left hand is dangerous. You know it could be it for him. But I just feel like Connor's just a I'm sorry. I'm just feel like Max is just a little too smart for that.

items.claim: Conor McGregor has maintained his balance and work ethic during his time away from competition.
items.quote: You forget that this man still has his balance. You forget that this man is willing to work. I don't think anyone's ever come out and said that he's lazy in the training room.

items.claim: A three-round fight would have been more favorable for Conor McGregor's return than a five-round fight.
items.quote: I would have liked this to be a three rounder for him coming back and not a five rounder. I would have loved a three rounder, but... for three rounds, I think Connor, yes, he's sharp enough and he can he's he's powerful enough in his mind that he could will himself through those three.
